file.content:
# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

content.thinking:
Now I have a good picture of the setup. Let me think about what the user is asking:

"how to utilize tmux sessionizer to have default sessions"

The current setup:
- `tpo` → `_tpo_function` → `tmux new-session -A -s primary`
- `tmo` → `_tmo_function` → `tmux new-session -A -s mobile`
- `tdo` → `_tdo_function` → `tmux new-session -A -s desktop`
- `two` → `_two_function` → `tmux new-session -A -s work`


They have the `tmux-sessionx` plugin bound to `o` for session management with fzf. A sessionizer is ThePrimeagen's concept—it uses fzf to pick from a directory list and creates or switches to a tmux session named after that directory. The user wants to leverage this pattern to automatically create or restore those four default sessions instead of manually triggering each one.

I'm thinking the best approach here is to either add a startup script in tmux.conf that auto-creates the default sessions when the server starts, or build a fish function that acts as a sessionizer for these named sessions. Given they already have tmux-resurrect/continuum for session restoration, they probably want a quick way to jump between or initialize these specific sessions on demand. I'm weighing the best approach here. The cleanest option is to add a check in fish's interactive shell initialization that creates the default sessions if they don't already exist, which keeps everything self-contained. I could also build a sessionizer function that combines predefined sessions with project directories and lets the user pick via fzf, or create a dedicated initialization script—though the shell init approach is simpler and doesn't require extra files.

Since they mentioned having `tmux-sessionx` already bound to `o`, I think what they're really after is automatic setup of those default sessions so the sessionizer has something to work with. Let me clarify the direction with them before writing out the full implementation. ePrimeagen-style sessionizer would:
1. List project dirs from ghq or ~/
2. Use fzf to select
3. Create session named after dir or switch to existing

But the user wants "default sessions" - meaning the named sessions they already have (primary, mobile, desktop, work).

I think the approach is:
1. Create a `_tmux_sessionizer` function that shows all default sessions + project dirs via fzf
2. Creates the session if it doesn't exist, switches to it if it does
3. The "default sessions" list would include primary, mobile, desktop, work
4. Also bind this to a tmux key (like `prefix + S` or similar)

Let me also think about the tmux-resurrect/continuum aspect - they already have `@continuum-restore 'on'` which restores sessions. But for default sessions to always exist, I need something more proactive. So I'll create a Fish function that maintains a hardcoded list of default sessions (primary, mobile, desktop, work) and optionally scans project directories, then uses fzf to select or create sessions. I can add a `tss` abbreviation for quick access and bind it to a tmux key, plus optionally bootstrap all default sessions on startup.

content.text: Now I have enough context. Let me write the plan.

input.content:
# Plan: Tmux Sessionizer with Default Sessions

## Context
The dotfiles already have 4 named tmux sessions managed individually via fish functions:
- `tpo` → `primary`, `tmo` → `mobile`, `tdo` → `desktop`, `two` → `work`

The goal is a **sessionizer** — an fzf-powered picker that lists default sessions, creates them if missing, and switches to them. This replaces manually typing `tpo`/`tmo` etc. and ensures default sessions always exist.

Existing relevant pieces:
- `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_t{po,mo,do,wo}_function.fish` — individual session attach fns
- `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` — shellAbbrs + xdg.configFile function list
- `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` — tmux keybinds, `tmux-sessionx` bound to `o`
- `tmux-resurrect` + `tmux-continuum` already configured for session persistence

## Implementation

### 1. New fish function: `_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish`

`home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish`

```fish
function _tmux_sessionizer_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then existing sessions not in default list
  set existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)
  test -z "$selected" && return

  if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
    end
  else
    tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
    else
      tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
    end
  end
end
```

### 2. Register abbreviation in `default.nix`

Add to `shellAbbrs`:
```nix
tss = "_tmux_sessionizer_function";
```

Add to the `xdg.configFile` function list:
```nix
"_tmux_sessionizer_function"
```

### 3. Bind to tmux key in `tmux.conf`

Add a keybind so it's accessible from inside tmux (replaces or complements `tmux-sessionx`):
```
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tmux_sessionizer_function'"
```

This opens the sessionizer in a temporary window, switches session, then closes.

## Files to Modify
- `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmux_sessionizer_function.fish` — **create**
- `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` — add abbr `tss` + register function
- `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` — add `bind S` keybind

## Verification
1. Run `tss` in fish shell → fzf list shows `primary mobile desktop work` + any existing sessions
2. Select a session that doesn't exist → it gets created and attached
3. Select an existing session → switches to it
4. Inside tmux: `prefix + S` → same picker opens in a new window
5. All existing `tpo`/`tmo`/`tdo`/`two` abbreviations continue working unchanged
